heading.title: Student File Management
heading.subtitle: Upload, download, and export student data and documents efficiently

info_note.title: Guide to Configuring and Exporting Student Data and Documents in a Compressed File
info_note.description:
This page allows you to generate a customized file containing student data by selecting the required fields from each section (Personal Information, Residence Details, Educational Information, Guardian Information, Housing Information, and Documents).

First, select the student type, nationality, residence type, or any other available filter criteria. The system will automatically display the relevant fields based on your selections to ensure accurate data.

You can choose which fields to include in the file by enabling the checkboxes, and you may deselect any field that is not required. The list of selected fields will be automatically updated in the sidebar.

Important note: You can reorder the selected fields from the sidebar using drag and drop so that the exported Excel file follows the same order you defined.

After selecting the required filters and fields, click the Export button. A compressed (ZIP) file will be downloaded, and it must be extracted to access the files inside.

After extracting the file, you will find:
- An Excel file containing student data according to the fields you selected.
- A Word file containing the students' national ID cards arranged and ready for printing.
- A folder named "students" that contains multiple subfolders, where each folder represents a single student.

Inside each student's folder, you will find the documents that were selected during the export process to be downloaded with that student's data.

When generating the Word file, the AI system runs automatically to process the national ID card images. The system first verifies that the uploaded image is a valid Egyptian national ID card, then automatically crops the card and adjusts its orientation and size to make it suitable for printing.

The Word file is organized so that each page contains data for a maximum of 3 students. The national ID number is displayed at the top, and below it two images are shown (the student's ID card and the guardian's ID card) arranged in two columns, making it suitable for printing on A4 paper.
